methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Hive AI is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Kevin Guo, currently valued at $2.0B post-money following $120M+ in cumulative fundraising. The peer set includes five same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, ElevenLabs, Labelbox) spanning $1.0B to $8.6B in valuation, plus one public reference point (PLTR).

Two valuation methods triangulate meaningfully here. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $2.0B Series D post-money, implying $3.0B at the next financing stage. Peer-median anchors to the five same-stage AI & Software comparables, which have a median valuation of $4.5B and a range of $1.0B to $8.6B. These methods produce a low estimate of $2.5B, median of $3.8B, and high estimate of $5.2B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.5B to $5.2B, with a midpoint near $3.8B. The spread reflects meaningful dispersion in the peer set—particularly the $8.6B valuation of Wayve versus the $1.0B floor at Labelbox—indicating that stage and sector alone do not fully explain valuation variance. Key uncertainties include product-market fit maturity, customer concentration, and unit economics relative to peers, all material drivers of upside or downside within the identified range.
